description: JSON schemas for QA (Quality Assurance) messages. QA is project-global. Identity: qa. One instance per project. QA reviews completed code packages against the subsystem specification. QA does not review code style, design language compliance, or implementation quality — that is CR's domain. QA asks one question: does this code do what the spec says it should do? Decisions are binary: approved or rejected.

common_fields.note: Every inbox message includes these fields

qa_approval.description: QA returns a binary spec-compliance verdict to SPM. Type code: QAP. When approved, qa_signoff_id equals this message's message_id and SPM uses it as qa_sign_off_id in task_completion. When rejected, qa_signoff_id is empty string.

type_specific_fields.in_response_to: string — CPK message_id of the code package being reviewed. Not the NTF that triggered the session — the actual CPK that contains the code.
type_specific_fields.cr_signoff_id: string — CR's approval message_id. QA verifies this is present and non-empty before proceeding. If empty, QA rejects immediately with reason: cr_signoff_missing.
type_specific_fields.spec_version: string — version of subsystem spec QA reviewed against e.g. vm_lifecycle_v1.3
type_specific_fields.spec_path: string — path to spec document used
type_specific_fields.spec_compliance_summary: string — one to three sentences. What QA checked and what the verdict means.
type_specific_fields.findings: array of qa_finding_object — empty if approved with no concerns. Non-empty for rejected, or for approved with non-blocking observations.
type_specific_fields.blocking_findings_count: integer — must be 0 if decision is approved
type_specific_fields.qa_signoff_id: string — equals message_id when approved, empty string when rejected

qa_finding_object.spec_section: string — the spec section the finding relates to
qa_finding_object.expected_behaviour: string — what the spec says should happen
qa_finding_object.observed_behaviour: string — what the code actually does based on the code package and test results
qa_finding_object.description: string — clear description of the discrepancy
qa_finding_object.suggested_resolution: string — may be empty; QA does not prescribe implementation

qa_approval.note: QA does not review code style, design language compliance, naming conventions, or implementation elegance. Those belong to CR. A finding that is not rooted in a specific spec section is not a valid QA finding.

spec_correction_request.description: QA sends this to SD when a spec ambiguity or error is discovered during review that cannot be resolved by reading the spec more carefully. QA cannot approve or reject code when the spec itself is the problem. Type code: SCR.

type_specific_fields.affected_spec: string — spec document name and version
type_specific_fields.affected_section: string — section of the spec that is ambiguous or incorrect
type_specific_fields.problem: string — precise description of the ambiguity or error
type_specific_fields.severity: string — enum: blocking | non_blocking. Blocking means QA cannot complete the review until resolved.
type_specific_fields.proposed_correction: string — QA's suggested resolution, may be empty
type_specific_fields.blocks_tasks: array of strings — task_ids whose QA review is suspended pending resolution
type_specific_fields.ppm_notified: boolean — always true; QA always notifies PPM when sending SCR

status_report.description: QA reports its state to PPM each cycle. Type code: SRP.

type_specific_fields.spec_correction_requests_sent: integer — derived: count of SCRs in spec_correction_requests array sent this cycle (completed_at within current cycle date)
type_specific_fields.spec_correction_requests_pending: integer — derived: count of spec_correction_requests entries with status: pending. Do not manually track — compute from the array each cycle.
type_specific_fields.rejection_patterns: array of strings — any recurring spec sections generating repeated rejections. Signals spec clarity issues.

escalation.description: QA escalates to PPM or PM when review cannot proceed. Type code: ESC.

type_specific_fields.category: string — enum: spec_missing | spec_ambiguous | code_package_unreadable | structural_concern
type_specific_fields.severity: string — enum: low | medium | high | critical
type_specific_fields.subject: string

convention_request.description: QA asks for a ruling on an uncovered situation. Type code: CVR.
